statement.en: COMPARATIVE CRITICAL ANALYSIS OF URBAN DESIGN PROCESSES IN TWO PLANNING SYSTEMS IN SAUDI ARABIA: CASE STUDIES OF THE WATERFRONT DEVELOPMENT IN JEDDAH AND YANBU
statement.summary: This research critically examines the urban design processes within two distinct planning systems in Saudi Arabia, focusing on the waterfront developments in Jeddah and Yanbu. It explores how decision-making environments, governance mechanisms, and the roles of various actors influence the quality of urban public spaces. Despite substantial urbanisation driven by development programs, research on design governance and the influence of the design process on urban space quality in Saudi Arabia remains limited. This gap necessitates a deeper understanding of how governance frameworks shape urban design outcomes and their alignment with national developmental objectives. Employing a comparative framework and a case study approach, the study evaluates the centralised, dependent planning system

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a provisional empirical case study (dated 2026, future/preprint status) examining urban design governance in Saudi Arabia. It presents comparative findings not yet validated through peer review or independent replication. The research makes substantive claims about causality between governance mechanisms and design outcomes, but these remain under investigation rather than established fact.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 都市空間の品質は、統治と行為者の力が時間を通じて織り交わるほど深まる。
